title: Crispy Cumin Lamb with Brothy Rice
summary: Cumin and lamb is one of the best flavour pairings. We've taken inspiration from the various Chinese cumin lamb dishes out there and combined elements of each to create this zingy and rich crispy cumin lamb with brothy rice. It's comforting and unbelievably delish.

method.description: Pick the coriander leaves, then transfer to a bowl of ice water. Thinly slice the spring onions, separating the greens and whites. Add the greens to the bowl of ice water with the coriander. Thinly slice the coriander stems and pick the basil leaves.

Place a wok over a high heat with a drizzle of oil. Once hot, add the mince and cook for 5-8 mins until browned and crispy. Stir through the coriander stems, spring onion whites, cumin seeds and szechuan peppercorns and cook for 1 min until fragrant.

Pour in the Shaoxing wine and let it evaporate completely. Add the oyster sauce and half the soy, then stir until glossy. Take off the heat and stir through the Thai basil until wilted. Transfer to a plate, then set aside.

Bash the ginger. Return the wok to a high heat. Once hot, add the ginger and let it sizzle for a couple of seconds. Pour over the stock, season with the remaining soy sauce and let it come to the boil.
Share the cooked rice between bowls, pour the broth over and top with the cumin lamb and herbs.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
